One of those companies is Perplexity, which took the stage with Intel to unveil the world's first "hybrid local/server agentic inference orchestrator." Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Collector Cars Enter a New Era

summary:
Hagerty Chairman and CEO McKeel Hagerty joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain how the company transformed from a niche insurance provider into a collector-car empire powered by media, memberships, and auctions. He discusses the coming $570 billion wealth transfer in collector vehicles, the surge in Ferrari values, and why electric vehicles could become the next sought-after collectible.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bitcoin’s Inflation-Hedging Promise in Tatters After 36% Plunge
title_cn: 暴跌 36% 比特币的通胀对冲承诺破灭
summary: Bitcoin has fallen 36% over the past year and slipped below $70,000 this week, extending a retreat that is undermining several of the arguments that helped carry the cryptocurrency into the financial mainstream.

title: First Brands Had '5 Red Flags,' Says Michael Gatto
title_cn: 迈克尔·加托 (Michael Gatto) 表示，第一品牌有“5 个危险信号”
summary:
The US government has joined the line of creditors impacted by alleged fraud at bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands. 
Columbia Business School Professor and Partner at Silver Point Capital, Michael Gatto discusses his take on the First Brands drama, as well as advice for young professionals in credit and distressed markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title_cn: 委内瑞拉希望石油公司为项目提供自己的电力
summary: Energy companies heading to Venezuela are being told to bring their own power plants to run their oil and natural gas operations and shield them from frequent blackouts on the nation’s ill-maintained electricity grid.

summary: Most emerging market currencies advanced as hopes of a deal in the Middle East boosted risk sentiment and US data showed a strong labor market.

title: Private Credit Eyes EasyJet Takeover
title_cn: 私人信贷关注易捷航空收购
summary: EasyJet's rejection of a reported Castlelake takeover approach sparks a bigger debate: Are UK-listed companies undervalued? Bloomberg's Silas Brown joins Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why a private credit giant is interested in aviation deals and reveals that private credit markets remain flooded with cash despite growing geopolitical and inflation concerns. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: HPE Rises on Sales Outlook; Victoria's Secret Soars on Profit Beat | Stock Movers
title_cn: HPE 因销售前景而上涨；维多利亚的秘密 (Victoria's Secret) 利润飙升 |库存搬运工
summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- Victoria's Secret (VSXY) shares are soaring after the lingerie maker, now trading under symbol VSXY, reports profit metrics and net sales for its first quarter that topped expectations, including adjusted operating income that was nearly twice the consensus estimate. Management boosted its annual forecasts. 
- Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) shares are higher in early trading after the company gave an outlook for annual sales that topped estimates, citing massive growth in AI-fueled demand for its servers and networking.
- Marvell Technology (MRVL) is rising after Nvidia Corp.'s Jensen Huang predicted the company will hit a $1 trillion valuation. Huang said Marvell's valuation will soar now that the age of "useful AI has arrived", and was joined by Marvell CEO Matt Murphy on stage at Computex trade show in Taipei. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How the 1987 Crash Changed the Life of Centerbridge's Jeff Aronson
title_cn: 1987 年的车祸如何改变了 Centerbridge 的 Jeff Aronson 的生活
summary: Jeff Aronson was working as a lawyer at LF Rothschild when the 1987 stock market crash put the firm out of business. In this conversation with David Rubenstein, the Centerbridge Co-Founder explains how that setback led him to a career in investing and eventually to Angelo Gordon. Aronson sits down with David Rubenstein for the premiere episode of Bloomberg Wealth Season 6. This interview was recorded April 28 in New York. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Pares Gains as US Jobs Data Reinforces Higher-Rate Bets
title_cn: 美国就业数据强化高利率押注，金价上涨
summary: Gold pared gains after robust US jobs data reinforced bets that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates higher for longer, a headwind for the precious metal.

title: Rheinmetall Wins Romanian Defense Contracts Worth €5.7 Billion
title_cn: 莱茵金属赢得罗马尼亚价值 57 亿欧元的国防合同
summary: Rheinmetall AG, Germany’s largest defense company, will provide €5.7 billion ($6.7 billion) worth of military gear to Romania to help shore up NATO’s Eastern flank, the firm’s biggest international contract package since the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

title: Chipmakers Outperform Software Stocks by Most on Record
title_cn: 芯片制造商的表现超越软件股，创历史新高
summary: Semiconductor stocks rallied on Tuesday while software fell, with the gap between the two sub-sectors of the tech industry the widest on record.

title: Blue Owl Tech Private Credit Fund Is Selling $300 Million Bonds
title_cn: Blue Owl Tech 私人信贷基金正在出售 3 亿美元债券
summary: A private credit fund managed by Blue Owl Capital Inc. is looking to sell $300 million of investment-grade bonds, according to a person familiar with the matter.

title: UAE’s Strait of Hormuz Pipeline Bypass May Also Include Fuels
title_cn: 阿联酋霍尔木兹海峡管道旁路可能还包括燃料
summary: The United Arab Emirates is considering building an additional pipeline for refined products that would bypass the Strait of Hormuz and carry fuels to the country’s east coast.

title: Brightline Fields Bankruptcy-Loan Bids While Hoping for a Suitor
title_cn: Brightline 进行破产贷款竞标，同时希望有收购者
summary: Brightline is weighing bankruptcy-loan offers from its largest creditors after the Florida passenger railroad failed to lure a buyer by a self-imposed bidding deadline last month, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Anthropic’s First-Mover IPO Edge Set to Widen Lead Over OpenAI
title_cn: Anthropic 的先行者 IPO 优势将扩大对 OpenAI 的领先优势
summary:
Anthropic PBC pulled ahead of OpenAI with its confidential IPO filing Monday, as the free-spending artificial intelligence startups battle for a fundraising edge that’s set to determine who will win the ultimate battle for computing power.

The oneupmanship in the firms’ private funding rounds, and now their progress toward going public, isn’t just about bragging rights. The risk for both firms is that the first to tap the US market’s unparalleled depth and liquidity will gain an immediate advantage in securing access to the chips, data centers and talent needed to build their AI models.

With Elon Musk looking to turbocharge SpaceX’s nascent AI offerings by strapping them to a hyperscaler with a chipmaker joint venture and doing an IPO, OpenAI and Anthropic can’t risk falling behind. Bloomberg Intelligence's Matthew Bloxham takes a closer look. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Indie Video Game ‘Mina the Hollower’ Sells 300,000 Copies in Three Days
title_cn: 独立视频游戏《Mina the Hollower》三天内售出 30 万份
summary: The new action-platformer is the best-reviewed video game of 2026 so far

title: Uber Caps Usage of AI Tools Like Claude Code to Cut Costs
title_cn: Uber 限制使用 Claude Code 等人工智能工具以削减成本
summary: Uber Technologies Inc. has set usage caps on some artificial intelligence-powered tools used by its staff, a move meant to manage costs after the company blew through its AI budget earlier this year.

title: SpaceX Wants Fee Cut From Bankers Chasing $500 Million Windfall
title_cn: SpaceX 希望银行家削减费用以获取 5 亿美元的意外之财
summary:
Elon Musk’s SpaceX is negotiating to pay razor-thin fees to Wall Street firms handling its IPO — but banks are still likely to rake in about $500 million from the record-setting market debut.

Musk’s space and artificial-intelligence conglomerate is negotiating to pay less than 0.75% for the $75 billion it aims to drum up in an initial public offering this month, according to people with knowledge of the matter. Even at that low spread, it will likely amount to one of the biggest fee events ever for Wall Street firms that arrange public listings.

The lead banks — Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and Morgan Stanley — are positioned to take in a bigger share of the fee pool than the other 21 brokers involved. For more, we speak with Sri Natarajan, Chief Wall Street Correspondent for Bloomberg News. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Perplexity Splits AI Work Between PCs and Servers to Ease Strain
title_cn: Perplexity 将 AI 工作分配到 PC 和服务器之间以缓解压力
summary: Perplexity AI Inc. is trying to manage the huge demand for artificial intelligence computing power by building a platform that diverts AI work between personal computers and cloud-based servers.

title: Vast, Axiom Look to Europe as NASA Space Station Plan in Limbo
title_cn: Axiom 将欧洲视为 NASA 空间站计划的悬而未决
summary: Vast and Axiom Space, both of which are developing commercial successors to the International Space Station, announced plans to expand into Europe as NASA signals it may no longer fund development of multiple future stations in Earth’s orbit.

title: EU’s AI Data Center Plans Stumble Due to Delays, Funding Issues
title_cn: 欧盟人工智能数据中心计划因延误和资金问题而陷入困境
summary: The European Union’s €20 billion ($23.3 billion) investment plan for five massive artificial intelligence data centers is floundering, with delays and funding issues alienating some potential partners, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: BofA Sees Strong African Deal Flow Despite Global Turmoil

summary: Bank of America Corp. expects strong deal-making activity in South Africa to continue this year despite ongoing global volatility, the head of its local operation said.

title: HPE Soars by Record After Raising AI-Fueled Sales Forecast
title_cn: 上调人工智能驱动的销售预测后，HPE 股价飙升创纪录
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. shares soared by the most ever after the company gave an outlook for annual sales that topped Wall Street’s estimates, citing massive growth in AI-fueled demand for its servers and networking.

title: Stocks Get Tech Lift Toward All-Time Highs
title_cn: 股票技术提振至历史新高
summary:
A revival of the artificial-intelligence trade kept fueling Wall Street momentum, with stocks also rising on hopes for an agreement that would end the war that has roiled financial markets around the world.
Francois Trahan, Chief Investment Strategist at BMO Capital Markets, discusses the macro forces that could alter market consensus. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Alphabet's Equity Pivot is Boost to Hyperscaler Credit
title_cn: Alphabet 的股权重心推动了超大规模信贷
summary:
Google parent Alphabet Inc. is raising $80 billion through a package of equity offerings, including an investment deal with Berkshire Hathaway Inc., to help fund ambitious and growing artificial intelligence spending plans.
Ted Mortonson, Managing Director: Technology at Baird, discusses this offering, the historic AI infrastructure buildout, and the companies powering the current tech cycle. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: HPE CEO Neri on Blowout AI Revenue Forecast, Pricing and Strategy
title_cn: HPE 首席执行官 Neri 谈井喷式 AI 收入预测、定价和战略
summary: HPE shares soared by the most ever after the company gave an outlook for annual sales that topped Wall Street’s estimates, citing massive growth in AI-fueled demand for its servers and networking. HPE CEO Antonio Neri joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech" to discuss the company's demand growth, memory prices and outlook. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Google's $80B AI Power Move
title_cn: 谷歌 80B 美元的 AI 力量举措
summary: Alphabet's surprise $80 billion capital raise sparked debate, but Jefferies Equity Research Analyst Brent Thill says it's a power move, not a rescue mission. He joins Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why Google is racing ahead of potential OpenAI, Anthropic and SpaceX IPOs, why AI spending could exceed $1 trillion annually and how the AI boom could fundamentally reshape corporate hiring and investment decisions. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: CEO Predicts Enterprise AI Surge
title_cn: CEO预测企业人工智能将激增
summary: MongoDB CEO CJ Desai joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down how AI could become a massive growth driver for the company. He says that every AI application needs a scalable data layer and reveals that his software company added 2,500 customers last quarter while targeting thousands more in the year ahead. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Meta’s Rosen, Former Head of Election Integrity, to Depart
title_cn: Meta 的前选举诚信负责人罗森即将离职
summary: Guy Rosen, Meta Platforms Inc.’s chief information security officer who previously oversaw the company’s election integrity work, is departing in the coming months.

title: Blue Origin’s Lengthy Launchpad Repair Extends SpaceX Dominance
title_cn: 蓝色起源漫长的发射台维修扩大了 SpaceX 的主导地位
summary: Blue Origin faces a formidable challenge that goes beyond simply replacing the New Glenn rocket it lost in last week’s explosion: rebuilding a destroyed launchpad that took years to construct.

title: Poland’s Leader Calls for Tech Sovereignty to Counter AI Risks
title_cn: 波兰领导人呼吁科技主权应对人工智能风险
summary: Poland needs to put technology sovereignty at the top of its political agenda to curb artificial intelligence-related risks threatening its security and economy, Prime Minister Donald Tusk said.

title_cn: Alphabet 将筹集 800 亿美元股权用于人工智能支出
summary: Google parent Alphabet Inc. is raising $80 billion through a package of equity offerings, including an investment deal with Berkshire Hathaway Inc., to help fund ambitious and growing artificial intelligence spending plans.

title: Marvell Soars After Nvidia Chief’s $1 Trillion Stock Call
title_cn: Nvidia 首席执行官 1 万亿美元股票认购后 Marvell 股价飙升
summary: Marvell Technology Inc. shares surged after Nvidia Corp.’s Jensen Huang predicted the semiconductor and networking company will be the next business to hit a $1 trillion valuation, more than five times its current market capitalization.

title: T-Mobile Wants to Sell a Lifestyle, Not Just Wireless
title_cn: T-Mobile 希望销售一种生活方式，而不仅仅是无线
summary: Felisha White, a 34-year-old T-Mobile US Inc. customer in New York, stays up late on Monday nights to see what the latest deal drops will be from her wireless provider.

title: How Lawsuits Alleging Social Media Harms Threaten Big Tech
title_cn: 指控社交媒体危害的诉讼如何威胁大型科技公司
summary: A flood of lawsuits filed in recent years against social media giants claim that by engineering their platforms to be hard to resist, the companies are causing serious harm to young users. In the first case to go to trial, a jury in March found Meta Platforms Inc. and Google negligent in the design and operation of their platforms — a landmark verdict.

title: Anthropic Offers Mythos Model Access to 150 Additional Groups
title_cn: Anthropic 为另外 150 个群体提供 Mythos 模型访问权限
summary: Anthropic PBC is allowing 150 additional organizations around the world to access Mythos, an artificial intelligence model aimed at finding cybersecurity vulnerabilities that the company has said was too dangerous to make available to the general public.

title: AI Voice Startup ElevenLabs Targets Warsaw Hub Expansion in Push for CEE Clients
title_cn: AI 语音初创公司 ElevenLabs 瞄准华沙中心扩建，以推动中东欧客户
summary: ElevenLabs sees Warsaw as its fastest-growing hub as the artificial intelligence voice company chases new customers in central and eastern Europe.

title: Alphabet's $80B Equity Raise & Trump Tries to Stop Israel Derailing Iran Deal | The Pulse 6/2/2026
title_cn: Alphabet 80B 美元融资和特朗普试图阻止以色列破坏伊朗协议 |脉搏 2026 年 6 月 2 日
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: John O'Toole, Amundi, CIO Solutions Global Head; Mariana Mazzucato, UCL, Economics of Innovation and Public Value Professor & Author, 'The Common Good Economy: A New Compass Author' & Anoush Ehteshami, Durham University, International Relations Professor.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Mariana Mazzucato: CEOs Should Be Contracted to Hit Goals
title_cn: Mariana Mazzucato：首席执行官应该签订合同以实现目标
summary: There's no lack of ambition to do good, says author Mariana Mazzucato, but the system is designed for us to fail. In her new book, The Common Good Economy: A New Compass, she argues for a goal-oriented economy, with targets written into CEOs' contracts. Mazzucato joined 'The Pulse with Francine Lacqua'. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Startup Impulse Space Raises $500 Million, Valued at $4 Billion
title_cn: Startup Impulse Space 融资 5 亿美元，估值 40 亿美元
summary: Impulse Space, a startup backed by billionaire Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund, raised $500 million in a new funding round, the company announced.

title: Withings Debuts New Smart Scale Marketed Toward GLP-1 Users
title_cn: Withings 首次推出面向 GLP-1 用户的新型智能体重秤
summary: Withings, the French company that pioneered the category of Wi-Fi bathroom scales, unveiled a new model with advanced body composition scanning abilities that is being marketed toward people on GLP-1 weight loss drugs.

title: US Tries to Stop Israel’s Lebanon Push Derailing Iran Deal
title_cn: 美国试图阻止以色列在黎巴嫩推动伊朗协议脱轨
summary: President Donald Trump is still optimistic the US can reach an interim peace deal with Iran soon, after the Islamic Republic threatened to suspend talks because of Israel’s escalating attacks in Lebanon.

title: Trump Signs AI Cybersecurity Order After Debate Over Its Reach
title_cn: 在对其影响范围进行争论后，特朗普签署了人工智能网络安全令
summary: President Donald Trump ordered US agencies to work with artificial intelligence companies on protecting against AI-enabled cyber attacks under a long-awaited directive that he had postponed last month citing his concern that it risked stifling tech innovation.

title: White House Correspondents Reschedule Dinner, Up Security
title_cn: 白宫记者重新安排晚餐时间并加强安保
summary: The White House Correspondents’ Association will hold another dinner on July 24, three months after its annual gala was postponed due to a shooting incident at the venue.

title: Stoltenberg: Oljefondet trenger teknologigantene
title_cn: 斯托尔滕贝格：石油基金需要科技巨头
summary: Finansminister Jens Stoltenberg intervjuet for Bloomberg Newsmakers

title: Burnham Says Won’t Call Early Election If Replaces Starmer
title_cn: 伯纳姆表示，如果取代斯塔默，不会提前举行选举
summary: Would-be UK prime minister Andy Burnham has ruled out a snap general election if he replaces Keir Starmer this summer, in an effort to tamp down speculation that Britain could be heading for an early vote.

title: France Moves Closer to Passing Bill to Help Its Food Industry
title_cn: 法国即将通过法案以帮助其食品工业
summary: France moved closer to passing an emergency bill to help expand livestock farming and tackle rising food imports, as the European agricultural powerhouse tries to revive its food trade balance.

title: Norway’s $2.3 Trillion Fund Needs to Stay Invested in Big Tech, Stoltenberg Says
title_cn: 斯托尔滕贝格表示，挪威 2.3 万亿美元的基金需要继续投资于大型科技公司
summary: Big tech and AI underpin weapons systems, Jens Stoltenberg says in a Bloomberg Newsmakers interview

title: Japan’s Takaichi Urges Passage of Vessels in Call With Iran
title_cn: 日本高市敦促与伊朗通话的船只通过
summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi called for the safe passage of all vessels regardless of nation in her third phone call with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian.

title: Norway Needs to Invest in Big Tech, Stoltenberg Says
title_cn: 斯托尔滕贝格表示，挪威需要投资大型科技
summary: Norway’s $2.3 trillion sovereign wealth fund needs ethics rules that allow it to stay invested in big tech stocks, Finance Minister Jens Stoltenberg said. He speaks to Bloomberg News Editorial Lead for EMEA Jackie Simmons at a Bloomberg Newsmakers event in Oslo. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Lowers Tariffs on Farm, Industrial Equipment as Costs Soar
title_cn: 由于成本飙升，美国降低了农业和工业设备的关税
summary: The White House announced it will reduce tariffs on imported farm and construction equipment such as harvesters and forklifts, an effort to boost the industrial economy and provide relief for American farmers.

title: Here Are Key Trump Officials Who Have Left the Administration
title_cn: 以下是已离开政府的特朗普主要官员
summary: A running list of officials who have left, why they exited and who replaced them.

title: Norway’s Stoltenberg Says NATO Is Now More Difficult to Manage
title_cn: 挪威斯托尔滕贝格表示北约现在更难管理
summary: NATO’s task is to keep US and Europe together, but there are limits, Stoltenberg says

title: Stoltenberg Weighs In on US-NATO Tensions, Wealth Fund
title_cn: 斯托尔滕贝格评论美国与北约的紧张局势和财富基金
summary: Former NATO chief Jens Stoltenberg says the tensions between the US and its European NATO allies are “more difficult to manage” now compared to when he left the alliance in 2024. Speaking with Bloomberg News Editorial Lead for EMEA Jackie Simmons at a Bloomberg Newsmakers event in Oslo, Stoltenberg also says it was “unacceptable” for the US to threaten Denmark, referring to President Donald Trump’s ambition to take Greenland. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Jeff Aronson Doesn't See a US Recession Coming Anytime Soon
title_cn: 杰夫阿伦森认为美国经济衰退不会很快到来
summary: Centerbridge Co-Founder Jeff Aronson says the US economy is "in reasonably good shape" and that he doesn't see a recession happening anytime soon. He also discusses the ripple effects war in the Middle East could have on markets if it persists, as well as his views on prediction markets. Aronson sits down with David Rubenstein for the premiere episode of Bloomberg Wealth Season 6. This interview was recorded April 28 in New York. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rubio Defends Iran War, Ebola Response in Combative Hearing
title_cn: 卢比奥在战斗听证会上为伊朗战争和埃博拉应对措施辩护
summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio defended the US war effort in Iran, as well as the Trump administration’s global public health strategy amid a worsening Ebola outbreak, as he faced hostile questions from Democrats in a hearing before Congress on Tuesday.

title: Trump Picks Loyalist Pulte as Acting Spy Chief After Gabbard
title_cn: 特朗普任命效忠派普尔特继加巴德之后担任代理间谍首长
summary: President Donald Trump appointed Bill Pulte, the head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, as the acting director of National Intelligence, elevating a loyalist with no national-security background to US spy chief.

title: US Pushing for 50% American Content Rule for Autos, Carney Says
title_cn: 卡尼表示，美国正在推动汽车 50% 的美国成分规则
summary: The White House is pushing for new rules on auto production that would require at least 50% US components for vehicles manufactured in the North American trade zone, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney confirmed.

title: Venezuela’s Rodríguez to Visit India as War Reshapes Oil Trade
title_cn: 战争重塑石油贸易，委内瑞拉总统罗德里格斯将访问印度
summary: Venezuela’s acting President Delcy Rodríguez will meet Prime Minister Narendra Modi this week, with energy security expected to dominate discussions as India seeks to diversify crude supplies disrupted by the Iran war.

title: Trump Names Pulte as Acting Director of National Intelligence
title_cn: 特朗普任命普尔特为代理国家情报总监
summary: President Donald Trump said he is tapping Bill Pulte, the head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, as the acting Director of National Intelligence. Pulte will keep running the FHFA as well. Bloomberg's Tyler Kendall reports.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Netanyahu Maneuvers Between Trump and Voters as Election Looms
title_cn: 选举临近之际，内塔尼亚胡在特朗普和选民之间进行调动
summary: Within hours of US President Donald Trump forcing Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to abandon a plan to bomb south Beirut, Israel’s parliament began preparing for elections — highlighting the combustible interplay between war and politics facing the premier.

title: Israel Defense Exports Set Fresh Record for Fifth Straight Year
title_cn: 以色列国防出口连续第五年创下新纪录
summary: Israel’s defense exports hit a record for a fifth consecutive year, with strong demand from Europe and the Asia-Pacific region driving sales.

title: UK Murder Case Becomes Flashpoint as Farage Faces Criticism
title_cn: 法拉奇面临批评，英国谋杀案成为爆发点
summary: The murder of an 18-year-old white student in Britain has become a political flashpoint as Reform UK leader Nigel Farage was criticized by senior Labour and Conservative politicians for his response to the case.

title: Russia Ships Most Oil Since 2022 as Drones Strike Refineries
title_cn: 无人机袭击炼油厂，俄罗斯自 2022 年以来运送了最多石油
summary: Year-to-date flows exceed averages for each year since Moscow’s troops invaded Ukraine in 2022

title: Magyar Hopes for Ukraine Deal This Week to Open EU Talks
title_cn: 马扎尔希望乌克兰本周达成协议以开启欧盟谈判
summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he hoped to close “technical” talks with Kyiv this week over a minority rights issue that is holding up the start of Ukraine’s formal negotiations to join the European Union.

title: Barred From Running Argentina, Kirchner Gets Her Own Pink Palace
title_cn: 基什内尔被禁止执掌阿根廷，却拥有了自己的粉红宫殿
summary: Cristina Kirchner may never return to Argentina’s pink presidential palace. But nearly a year after being barred for life from running for public office, her loyal fans brought the palace to her.

title: At Least 18 Killed in Russian Missile Attack on Ukraine
title_cn: 俄罗斯导弹袭击乌克兰 至少18人死亡
summary: Russia carried out a large-scale drone and missile strike on Ukraine, killing at least 18 people and injuring more than a hundred.

title: What You Need to Know About California's Primary Elections
title_cn: 关于加州初选您需要了解什么
summary: The primary for California governor is underway, and it’s a nail-biter. Eliyahu Kamisher breaks down what you need to know about the election. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Reality TV Star, Ex-Fox News Host Emerge as Serious GOP Contenders in California Primaries
title_cn: 真人秀明星、前福克斯新闻主持人成为加州初选共和党的有力竞争者
summary: Steve Hilton paced the stage at a brewery outside San Francisco with a simple message: California is broken and only an outsider can fix it.

title: Ghana Plans SOE Reforms, Forson Says
title_cn: 福森表示，加纳计划进行国有企业改革
summary: Ghana Finance Minister Cassiel Ato Forson said the government plans reforms of state-owned enterprises. He spoke to Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja in London. (Source: Bloomberg)
